response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has meaningful geospatial and GIS experience through GISCollective and Open Green Map, and some exposure to high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby, but his primary language stack is TypeScript/JavaScript/Node.js and DLang — neither C++ nor Rust, which are the core requirements for this role. His only C++ work was a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, neither of which reflects the depth of commercial systems programming needed for routing tile pipelines. There is no evidence of experience with navigation systems, routing data pipelines, or real-time traffic data integration.
strengths: Genuine geospatial platform experience: built GISCollective, contributed to Open Green Map, and presented at FOSS4G 2019
Distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of low-latency HTTP requests per second with async I/O
Broad cross-stack adaptability and consistent track record of owning complex systems end-to-end across many roles
concerns: No current or recent C++ or Rust proficiency — last C++ work was 2014–2015, limited to tooling and a thesis project
Core expertise is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, which are misaligned with the systems-level language requirements of this role
No experience with routing data pipelines, navigation tile generation, or real-time traffic signal integration
